description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous artisan workshop (atelier) interior, rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO triptych frames, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders between any parts — strictly forbidden to split the image into thirds or sections visually; the result must read as a single oil-painted scroll-like view of the same room.

ABSOLUTELY CRITICAL compositional constraint: this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally and snaps to one of three viewing windows (0–33%, 33–66%, 66–100% of the width). For clean swipes, NO furniture, NO door, NO window, NO shelving unit, NO architectural feature MAY CROSS OR STRADDLE the vertical positions at exactly 33% or 66% of the image. Every major object must be contained ENTIRELY within ONE third. The areas around 33% and 66% (roughly 31–35% and 64–68%) must contain ONLY plain continuous wall surface — these are the 'breathing zones' where one viewing window transitions to the next.

Architecture (described continuously across the width): the viewer looks into a warm artisan's workshop with three walls visible. At the far LEFT (roughly 0–18%), a LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. From 18% to 82% we see the FLAT BACK WALL straight on. From 82% to the right edge, a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. The walls meet at TWO interior corners (around 18% and 82%) — visible only as a soft change of wall plane and gentle painted shading at the join, never as a hard line. ONE continuous old wide-plank wooden floor runs unbroken across the bottom, slightly worn and uneven. ONE continuous exposed wooden ceiling beam runs unbroken across the top. The walls are warm aged plaster with occasional exposed vertical wooden framing posts (timber studs).

Feature details by horizontal position (0% = left edge, 100% = right edge):

• 4–28% — SECTION 1 (LEFT): on the BACK WALL, a WOODEN GARDEN DOOR centered around 16%, fully contained within Section 1 (right edge of door frame must be left of 30%, leaving ≥3% of plain wall before the 33% breathing zone). The door is painted in a soft sage-green tone with old patina, brass handle, and glass panels in the upper half divided by simple wooden mullions. Through the glass we see a SUNLIT GARDEN view: rosebushes in full bloom, climbing vines on a low stone wall, a paved garden path, distant chestnut trees, warm afternoon sunlight filtering through foliage. Painted in the same Art Déco line-art + soft wash style. Immediately in front of the door, a small worn rope doormat lies on the floor. The floor around the door is otherwise EMPTY (no furniture, no objects).

• 31–35% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain continuous plaster wall, no door frame edge, no window, no furniture, no decorative element. The 33% mark must be empty wall.

• 36–63% — SECTION 2 (CENTER): on the BACK WALL, a substantial PROFESSIONAL ARTISAN WORKBENCH (établi de menuisier / joiner's workbench), CENTERED at around 50%, fully contained within Section 2 (left edge at ≥38%, right edge at ≤62%). This is a REAL workshop workbench: sturdy heavy construction, ~1.5m wide, ~85–90cm tall (standard working height — the top is at about hip level for an adult standing), THICK worn-beech or worn-oak top (8–10 cm thick slab), four heavy square legs with sturdy horizontal stretchers/cross-bars near the floor, and a CAST-IRON BENCH VICE (étau) bolted to the front-left corner of the top surface with visible turning handle. The workbench top is COMPLETELY EMPTY (apart from the integrated vice) — no tools, no objects, no clutter — just bare worn wood ready to receive composited items later. Above the workbench, mounted on the back wall and CENTERED around 50%, a WOODEN WINDOW fully contained within Section 2 (left edge ≥38%, right edge ≤62%). The window's sill rests about 20cm above the workbench top. The window has an Art Nouveau wooden frame: the upper portion is a decorative IMPOSTE (transom) with curved, organic, stylized Art Nouveau wooden mullions forming gentle whiplash curves and stylized plant tendrils. The main panes below the imposte are divided by simple straight vertical wooden mullions (3 vertical panes). Through the glass we see the SAME SUNLIT GARDEN as Section 1: rosebushes, climbing vines, stone wall, paved path, chestnut foliage. A polished wooden WINDOW LEDGE / TABLETTE (tablette en bois) runs along the interior side of the windowsill, slightly protruding into the room, intended to hold flower pots — but the tablette is COMPLETELY EMPTY for now.

• 64–68% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain continuous plaster wall, no window, no shelf, no furniture, no decorative element. The 66% mark must be empty wall.

• 69–96% — SECTION 3 (RIGHT): fully contained within Section 3. Layout: on the BACK WALL, a SMALLER ARTISAN WORKBENCH (petit établi) centered around 76%, fully contained within Section 3 (left edge ≥70%, right edge ≤82%). Same real workshop construction as the central workbench but narrower: ~0.85m wide, same ~85–90cm working height, thick worn-wood top, sturdy square legs with stretchers. Top is COMPLETELY EMPTY. Above this small workbench, a SECOND WOODEN WINDOW (smaller, fully contained within Section 3, centered around 76%, left edge ≥70%, right edge ≤82%) with the same Art Nouveau imposte design (curving organic wooden mullions in the upper transom, straight vertical mullions in the main panes below, 2 vertical panes). The window's sill rests about 20cm above the small workbench top. Through this window we see the SAME continuous garden view. The tablette en bois ledge continues under this window too, also EMPTY. To the RIGHT of the small workbench, against the back wall and partially overlapping onto the angled right side wall, a TALL EMPTY METAL SHELVING UNIT (étagère métallique industrielle), centered around 89%, fully contained within Section 3 (left edge ≥84%, right edge ≤96%). The shelving unit is industrial vintage: dark forest-green painted steel angle-iron frame (riveted or welded), with FOUR HORIZONTAL EMPTY metal shelves at evenly spaced heights from near the floor up to about 1.8m tall, light surface patina and faint rust on edges. All four shelves are COMPLETELY EMPTY — naked metal, ready to receive objects later.

• 96–100% — RIGHT SIDE WALL angling forward beyond the shelving unit. Plain warm plaster with one exposed vertical wooden framing post. Empty floor and empty wall.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, plus secondary sources from the two back-wall windows. Consistent across the whole panorama. Cohesive Art Déco rendering: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain. No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINTS:
(a) The TWO garden views (through the door at left, through the two back-wall windows) all show the SAME continuous garden seen at slightly different angles — rosebushes, climbing vines, stone wall, paved path, chestnut foliage, warm afternoon light. Consistent palette and brushwork.
(b) No transparent areas anywhere — the PNG is fully opaque.
(c) No vertical seams, no black bars, no panel borders anywhere in the image.
(d) Workbenches, tablettes, and metal shelves are ALL COMPLETELY EMPTY — no tools, no objects, no clutter, no jars, no pots, no flowers, no boxes. Just bare wood and bare metal waiting for composited items.
(e) ABSOLUTELY NO furniture, NO door, NO window, NO shelving unit, NO architectural feature crosses or straddles the vertical positions at 33% or 66% — those positions are strictly empty plaster wall.
(f) Workbenches are REAL workshop workbenches (not desks, not tables, not coffee tables): thick wooden top at standard working height (~85–90cm = hip-level for a standing adult), sturdy heavy legs with stretchers, the central one having a bolted-on cast-iron bench vice.
(g) The shelving in Section 3 is METAL (steel angle-iron, industrial vintage, dark green painted), NOT wood.
